user_query: As a nostalgic former CII engineer from the 1970s, I’d like a small event-driven folder-watcher that helps me build a tidy dossier whenever I drop a new batch of topics to research. Please implement an automation that monitors the input/ directory for new JSON batch files with the schema shown in input/cii_targets.json. On startup, process any pending batches and then keep watching for new ones. For each batch file: 1) Read the JSON and iterate targets[]. Each target has a Wikidata QID and a free-text note. 2) For each QID, download the corresponding entity JSON from the official Wikidata entity API on the wikidata.org domain (use the QID to identify the resource; no mirrors). Save the unmodified response as outputs/{batch_id}/raw/{qid}.json. 3) From each downloaded entity JSON, extract a structured summary with these fields: qid; label_en; description_en; aliases_en_pipe (join English aliases with ' | '); inception_year (earliest year from P571 if present); manufacturer_ids_pipe (IDs from P176 joined with ' | '); instance_of_ids_pipe (IDs from P31 joined with ' | '); image_filename (P18 if present); enwiki_title (sitelinks.enwiki.title if present); keyword_match (true if the target's note appears, case-insensitive, as a substring of any of label_en, description_en, any alias, or enwiki_title; otherwise false); status ('ok' if downloaded and parsed, otherwise 'error'); error (short message if status='error', else empty). Write one row per target to outputs/{batch_id}/summary.csv. 4) Maintain a simple state file at state/processed_batches.json to avoid reprocessing the same batch_id, and append a one-line entry to logs/run.log with timestamp, batch_id, and counts of ok/error rows. Behavior requirements: - The watcher must handle at least the provided input/cii_targets.json and produce the outputs in the specified paths. - Treat network failures or missing entities robustly: still write a summary row with status='error' and an error message, and create the batch folder even if some downloads fail. - Do not use hardcoded URLs beyond identifying the official Wikidata entity API by QID on wikidata.org. Deliverables that I can inspect locally: outputs/{batch_id}/summary.csv, outputs/{batch_id}/raw/*.json files, state/processed_batches.json, and logs/run.log. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
